text.title: AI-Agile Agent Workflow and Patterns
text.summary: Sprint planning and retrospective agent patterns, prompt structures, CLI tools, and common pitfalls when integrating Claude with Agile PM APIs.

section.title: Agentic Workflow
section.p: Claude subagents enhance Agile by operating between ceremonies: reading current sprint board state, identifying blockers and overdue tasks, generating draft user stories from product briefs, and producing retrospective summaries. All agent outputs are starting points for team discussion — not final decisions. The Scrum Master remains human-in-the-loop for all ceremony facilitation.

rule.statement: Present agent-generated retrospective summaries without AI attribution in the ceremony — teams that know content is AI-generated engage less critically with it.
rule.rationale: Attribution primes teams to accept or reject wholesale rather than engaging analytically with the content.

rule.statement: Configure AI story creation to always include a "so that [business value]" clause — AI-generated stories without business value context create technical tasks masquerading as user stories.
rule.rationale: The benefit clause is the hardest part for models to generate from code context alone; it requires product intent input from humans.

section.title: Prompt Patterns
section.reference: Sprint planning input generator with story breakdown, ACs, DoD

section.title: CLI Tools
section.p: jira-cli: sprint management, issue creation, board queries (github.com/ankitpokhrel/jira-cli).
linear-cli: cycle management in Linear (no official CLI; use GraphQL API directly).
gh: GitHub Projects board management, PR status. Best agent interface for GitHub-based Agile.
curl + jq: direct API calls for sprint data extraction.
section.reference: Fetch active cycle tasks from Linear GraphQL API

antipattern.description: Using AI work breakdown for niche domains (embedded systems, ML pipelines) without customization.
antipattern.reason: AI work breakdown is trained on general software patterns; domain-specific subtask suggestions are irrelevant and mislead planning.

antipattern.description: Mixing velocity predictions from vendor AI (ClickUp Brain, Jira) with Claude inferences in the same report.
antipattern.reason: The models are different; unlabeled mixed predictions confuse stakeholders and corrupt trend analysis.

antipattern.description: Running AI velocity prediction on fewer than 6 sprints of history.
antipattern.reason: Predictions require calibrated patterns; new teams get noise, not signal — and act on it.

rule.statement: When assigning issues to a sprint (cycle) in Linear via GraphQL mutation, fetch the current cycle ID first — mutations require the ID, not the cycle name.
rule.rationale: Agents that use cycle names in mutations receive 400 errors or silently assign to the wrong cycle.
